title: New, non-underware hat logo artwork (replaces 357)
body: Submitting for @mairin #357 to get past signature issues.

comments.body: I'd hope to just update #357, but couldn't figure out the git magic.  This one too has my prior touchups to the scripts.

:pushpin: Commit 86db83d has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 86db83d65dea6d6a7a19f59c47495622a36c9b58 -->
Git is still looking for a signature on @mairin 's original commit in this commit.  Trying to figure out how to make it happy without luck so far.
OK, past the DCO issue, I needed to squish @mairin's commit and that looks to have made git happy.  Git learning of the day for me.

:pushpin: Commit 46601e2 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 46601e26e59948b155b226b0bda5c6e17b57125d -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
FWIW, starting a `git rebase -i`, and then specifying the `reword` command instead of the default `pick` for the unsigned commits, would offer an opportunity to manually edit the commit messages.
